question: What is the latest stellar-core release and which protocol version does it target?

golden.answer: As of **2026-07-09**, the latest stable stellar-core release is **v27.1.0** (published 2026-06-25), a v27-series release supporting **Protocol 27 / CAP-0071**. Treat the exact patch tag as volatile and confirm it against GitHub releases. Software release and network activation are distinct milestones: v27.1.0 shipped before Mainnet activated P27, and live Horizon shows the activation at ledger **63386819** on **2026-07-08 17:00:10 UTC**. The durable lesson is not that P27 remains pending; it is that a release tag alone never proves current network state. Check both the release feed and the live network.
keyFacts: Identifies the latest stellar-core release as a v27-series release targeting Protocol 27 (CAP-0071) — any dated, sourced v27.x tag is acceptable (v27.1.0, published 2026-06-25, as of 2026-07-03); never require one frozen tag.
Flags the tag and network status as separate freshness-sensitive facts, using GitHub releases for the tag and live Horizon/RPC for Mainnet activation.
avoid: Do NOT assert an old stellar-core release as the latest without checking the live release feed.
Do NOT claim Protocol 27 is still pending on Mainnet; it activated on 2026-07-08.
Do NOT infer network activation from a software release alone or present the exact patch tag as permanent.
golden.notes:
Freshness item (extra). RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-29: stellar-core v27.0.0 (targets Protocol 27 / CAP-0071) is the latest release — developers.stellar.org Software Versions lists Stellar Core 27.0.0 under 'Protocol 27 (Testnet, June 18, 2026)'. P26 (Stellar Core 26.1.0) is the live Mainnet version; P27 Mainnet vote scheduled 2026-07-08 (still future). Confidence medium = freshness-sensitive. Trap is conflating 'released' with 'activated on Mainnet'.
Also good if the answer: Notes that the release targeting Protocol 27 is ahead of Mainnet activation (P26 was the live Mainnet version at the snapshot; the P27 vote was pending).
Golden cites: The stellar-core GitHub releases page (github.com/stellar/stellar-core/releases).
Corrected 2026-07-03 for the v27.1.0 tag, then re-verified post-vote 2026-07-09 (todo 831): P27 is now live. Preserve only the durable distinction that release and activation require different sources; never grade 'P27 is live' as wrong.

corroboration.claim: Latest stellar-core release is v27.1.0 (published 2026-06-25), not v27.0.0

rootCause: eval-side authoring: single-source-class 're-verification' — the 06-29 check read the docs Software Versions page (a protocol-support table) instead of the GitHub releases feed the golden itself names as its live source, missing the 06-25 patch release; exactly the class-independence failure the golden-truth skill forbids
